Let's begin · Trip overview
At a Glance

Your Trip in One Look

Welcome — here's the shape of the trip. The Las Vegas bookends are locked in: one night downtown at Golden Nugget on the way out, one night at Circa on the way back. The middle — your seven nights in Hawaii — is where you have a real choice to make. Three different Hawaii experiences are below, each a different style of trip. Pick the one that sounds like you, and we'll dial in the specific hotels together.

Next: Your Vegas bookend nights
Las Vegas · Bookend Nights

Vegas — Outbound & Return

Two downtown nights, by design — same Fremont Street neighborhood but very different vibes. Going out at the Golden Nugget — old-Vegas energy, the famous shark-tank pool, big rooms. Coming back at Circa — the newest property downtown, adults-only, with the biggest pool deck in the city. Both within a five-minute walk of each other on Fremont. Both are locked in — no decision needed here.
Outbound · Day 1
Golden Nugget Las Vegas
129 E. Fremont Street · Famous shark-tank pool · Free self-parking

Carson Tower, Grand Room — the calmer choice within the property, on the Diamond-Head-end of the building. Strip views from upper floors. The Fremont Street light show runs nightly right outside; we'll request a higher floor away from the canopy for quiet.

Return · Day 9
Circa Resort & Casino
8 Fremont Street · Adults-only (21+) · Stadium Swim · Legacy Club rooftop

The newest tower downtown and the only adults-only resort in Las Vegas. Stadium Swim is the headline — six heated pools, a 143-foot screen, swim-up bars — and after seven days at the beach, ending on a rooftop pool with sportsbook drinks is the right note. Legacy Club on top of the building is one of the best sunset cocktails in the city.

Why this combination works
Both properties are downtown, both walk to Fremont Street Experience, both have free or low-cost parking. The Golden Nugget delivers classic Vegas character on night one when you're tired from travel; Circa delivers the resort wind-down on the way home when you've been on island time and want something calmer than the Strip but more polished than your average downtown hotel.
Next: Your three Hawaii experiences
Hawaii · Three Experiences

Pick Your Hawaii

Hawaii isn't one trip — it's six islands and a dozen styles of vacation. Below are three different Hawaii experiences, each tuned to a different kind of week. Read each one through and pick whichever sounds like the trip you want. Once you tell me which experience speaks to you, I'll come back with two specific hotel options inside that style — at the right price and the right neighborhood. The pricing shown for each is a starting estimate for that experience style, including 7 nights of accommodations.
Start with Experience A · Classic Waikiki
Experience A

Classic Waikiki

A
The Iconic Hawaii Trip
Classic Waikiki
Honolulu · Oahu · Walk-everywhere beach week

Wake up to Diamond Head, walk to the beach in flip-flops, finish the day with a mai tai at Duke's. The original picture-postcard Hawaii trip — built around walkability, sunset, and the easy rhythm of one neighborhood you don't need to leave.

Day 2
Land in Honolulu, transfer to Waikiki, sunset stroll on the beach, easy first dinner.
Day 3
Beach day. Pool day. Sunset cocktails at House Without a Key with live hula on the lawn.
Day 4
Sunrise hike up Diamond Head. Afternoon at the Royal Hawaiian Center for hula and lei-making classes.
Day 5
Pearl Harbor & USS Arizona Memorial — half-day from Waikiki. Quiet evening dinner.
Day 6
Hanauma Bay snorkeling morning. Afternoon shopping along Kalakaua Avenue.
Day 7
Open beach day or optional Paina Waikiki Luau in the evening.
Day 8
Last morning swim. Brunch at Orchids. Pack up and head to HNL.
Best Fit If You Want
A trip where you don't have to drive — everything is walkable from your hotel. Built-in evening atmosphere from the surrounding neighborhood. Easy to pivot from a beach day to dinner without changing plans. The most "I went to Hawaii" of the three options.
Where You'll Stay
Mid-tier Waikiki property
Near beach · ~$280–340/night range · Two finalist hotels TBD
Trade-off to Know
Crowded by design
Waikiki is busy — that's the point. Quiet beach is not on this menu.
STARTING FROM · 7 NIGHTS · WAIKIKI
TAP HERE TO PICK CLASSIC WAIKIKI
from$2,095
~$299/night + taxes
Next: Experience B · Two-Island Adventure
Experience B

Two-Island Adventure

B
More than One Island
Oahu & Maui
A few days each · Honolulu energy + Maui quiet

A short Honolulu stop for the icons — Pearl Harbor, Diamond Head, one good Waikiki sunset — then a quick inter-island flight to Maui for the rest of the week. Two completely different Hawaiis in one trip.

Day 2
Land Honolulu. Sunset on Waikiki Beach. Easy dinner walking distance.
Day 3
Pearl Harbor in the morning. Diamond Head climb in the late afternoon.
Day 4
Short morning flight HNL → OGG (Maui). Afternoon at the Maui resort. Sunset on Kaanapali or Wailea.
Day 5
Road to Hana — full day. Waterfalls, black sand beach, banana bread stops.
Day 6
Snorkel Molokini Crater (boat morning). Pool afternoon.
Day 7
Haleakala summit at sunrise (optional). Beach day. Lahaina-area dinner.
Day 8
Slow morning. OGG → HNL → LAS for the Vegas return night.
Best Fit If You Want
Two distinctly different Hawaiis in one trip. The big-name attractions (Pearl Harbor, Diamond Head) plus the genuinely relaxed island feel that Waikiki doesn't deliver. You're willing to pack and unpack twice for the variety.
Where You'll Stay
2 nights Waikiki + 5 nights Maui
Resort-style Maui property in Kaanapali or Wailea
Trade-off to Know
One extra travel day
Inter-island flight is short (~30 min) but adds an airport day in the middle.
STARTING FROM · 7 NIGHTS · OAHU + MAUI
TAP HERE TO PICK TWO-ISLAND
from$3,795
~$542/night + taxes & inter-island flight
Next: Experience C · Big Island Retreat
Experience C

Big Island Retreat

C
The Quiet Hawaii
Kona & the Big Island
Volcanoes, manta rays, lava-rock coast · One resort, all week

Skip the Waikiki crowds entirely. Land on the Kona coast and stay in one resort all week — the quieter, more elemental Hawaii of black lava fields, sea-turtle beaches, and the only night-snorkel with manta rays in the world. Slower mornings, fewer cars, bigger sunsets.

Day 2
Fly LAS → KOA (Kona). Resort check-in. Sunset at the resort beach.
Day 3
Manta ray night snorkel — the signature Big Island experience. Beach morning.
Day 4
Hawaii Volcanoes National Park — full day. Kilauea overlook, lava tube walk.
Day 5
Pool day. Spa afternoon. Sunset cocktails at the resort.
Day 6
Punaluu black sand beach + green sea turtles. Coffee farm tour in Kona.
Day 7
Snorkel Kealakekua Bay (Captain Cook). Last beach evening.
Day 8
Slow morning. KOA → LAS for Vegas return night.
Best Fit If You Want
Genuinely quiet — fewer crowds than Waikiki by an order of magnitude. One unpack, one resort. A more nature-driven trip with the most uniquely Hawaiian experiences (manta rays at night, active volcano, black sand). Less to walk to off-property — your resort is the village.
Where You'll Stay
Big Island resort · Kona-Kohala coast
All-in-one property with multiple pools, spa, beach, dining
Trade-off to Know
Rental car required
Resort is remote — no walk-to dining or shopping district. You'll drive to do anything off-property.
STARTING FROM · 7 NIGHTS · BIG ISLAND
TAP HERE TO PICK BIG ISLAND
from$3,495
~$499/night + taxes & rental car
